title: Quality Communication using Varying Parameters for Wireless Temporary Ad hoc Networks
author: K. Thamizhmaran
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A little packet known as a beacon packet is transmitted; it signals the existence of a base station, and mobile units detect the beacons and make an effort to connect wirelessly. Geographic routing methods have gained a lot of attention recently because they solely base their forwarding decisions on the local topology. MANET nodes can be identified by their limited resources, which include storage, bandwidth, remaining energy, routing coordination, end-to-end latency, and multi-path communication. Because of topology, MANET routing is a significant problem.   This work builds upon my previously published paper and proposes multipath work with three distinct topology sizes, three distinct node sets, and three distinct malicious node sets. It uses Network Simulator 2 (NS2) to simulate a variety of parameters, including packet delivery ratio, throughput, routing overhead, packet loss, delay, and remaining energy.
